'''Anii Degarienne''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]].

==Biography==
Anii Degarienne was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] and the wife of [[Matt Turhaya]], son of the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] hero [[Arada Turhaya]]. Degarienne encouraged Turhaya to follow his dreams of serving in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]; Turhaya attended the [[Imperial Naval Academy/Legends|Imperial Naval Academy]] and graduated at the top of his class. A few [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] after his graduation, Degarienne was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in a freak accident at the weapons manufacturing plant she worked at. Turhaya took their daughter, [[Alexandra Winger|Alexandra]], to live with relatives on [[Janara III]] while he served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Turhaya quickly became disillusioned with the Empire, and despised its methods and actions, eventually deserting from the service. He made his way to Janara III a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] later, only to find that the planet had been [[Raid on Janara III|raided]] by Imperial forces<ref name="P" /> commanded by [[High Inquisitor]] [[Adalric Cessius Brandl]].<ref name="Betrayal">{{JournalCite|12|Betrayal by Knight}}</ref> Turhaya found no survivors of the raid, and believed Alexandra killed.<ref name="P" /> She had survived, however, and was taken to the planet [[Garos IV]], where she was fostered with the planet's Imperial [[Governor/Legends|governor]], [[Tork Winger]] and his wife, [[Sali Winger|Sali]].<ref name="TP">{{JournalCite|5|Turning Point (Charlene Newcomb)}}</ref> Alexandra held some memories of her mother, although she believed her name was "Ana."<ref name="GH">{{JournalCite|1|A Glimmer of Hope}}</ref>
